docs.title: PRISM
docs.text:
Platform for Reliability, Intelligence, Strategy & Maturity

A unified framework for capability-driven organizational intelligence, connecting what you need (capabilities), how you measure (maturity), and how you act (execution).

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Module Purpose Documentation prism-core Shared primitives Domain, Layer, Stage, MaturityLevel prism-capability What capabilities exist Capability stacks, layers prism-maturity How we measure maturity SLIs, SLOs, maturity models prism-roadmap How we improve OKRs, roadmaps, PRDs prism (this repo) Orchestration Cross-module queries

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/grokify/prism@latest

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "github.com/grokify/prism/ecosystem"
)

func main() {
    // Create an ecosystem
    eco := ecosystem.New(ecosystem.Config{
        Name: "my-organization",
    })

    // Load capability stacks
    eco.LoadCapabilityStack("capabilities/security.json")

    // Load maturity documents
    eco.LoadPRISMDocument("maturity/model.json")

    // Query across modules
    stats := eco.Statistics()
    fmt.Printf("Capabilities: %d\n", stats.TotalCapabilities)
    fmt.Printf("Metrics: %d\n", stats.TotalMetrics)
}

docs.title: Gap-Driven Planning
docs.text:
1. Capability Stack defines what the organization needs
  2. Maturity Model defines how well each capability should perform
  3. Maturity State tracks where we are today
  4. Execution Plan defines how we improve

docs.title: Maturity Levels (M1-M5)
docs.text: Level Name Characteristics M1 Reactive Missing or informal, inconsistent outcomes M2 Basic Some tooling, individual-dependent M3 Defined Standardized, repeatable across teams M4 Managed Measured, governed, integrated M5 Optimizing Continuously improved, adaptive

docs.title: Site Generator
docs.text:
Generate static sites from capability stacks with maturity visualization:

prism site generate --stack=./stacks/ --output=./dist

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Metrics Count Indicator - Badge showing SLI count per capability
  • Light/Dark Theme - Automatic theme synchronization
  • Navigation - Browse by category or layer
  • Custom Footer - Use --hide-generated-by to remove attribution

docs.title: CLI Options
docs.text: Flag Description --stack Path to capability stack directory --output Output directory for generated site --hide-generated-by Hide footer attribution

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Ecosystem Design - Full architecture documentation
  • v0.7.0 Release Notes - Latest release

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
PRISM is a unified framework for capability-driven organizational intelligence, connecting what you need (capabilities), how you measure (maturity), and how you act (execution).

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           prism                            │
│                Unified Orchestration Layer                 │
│      Queries, cross-references, dashboards, workflows      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                    │                    │
         ▼                    ▼                    ▼
┌────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐    ┌───────────────┐
│prism-capability│    │  prism-maturity │    │ prism-roadmap │
│                │    │                 │    │               │
│ "What we need" │───>│ "How we measure"│───>│ "How we act"  │
│                │    │                 │    │               │
│ Capabilities   │    │ Maturity Models │    │ OKRs/V2MOM    │
│ Layers         │    │ SLI/SLO Defs    │    │ Roadmaps      │
│ Categories     │    │ Current State   │    │ Initiatives   │
│ Dependencies   │    │ Gap Analysis    │    │ Requirements  │
└────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘    └───────────────┘

docs.title: Flow Summary
docs.text: Stage Artifact Question Answered Module 1 Capability Stack What capabilities do we need? prism-capability 2 Maturity Model + State Where are we for each capability? prism-maturity 3 OKR/V2MOM + Roadmap What goals and sequence to improve? prism-roadmap 4 MRD/PRD/TRD How do we execute each roadmap item? prism-roadmap

docs.title: Relationships
docs.text:
• Capability → Maturity: Each capability links to SLIs via PRISMRef.SLIIDs
  • Maturity → Roadmap: Gaps between current state and target drive roadmap priorities
  • Roadmap → Requirements: Each Roadmap Item (RMI) is executed via MRD/PRD/TRD documents

docs.title: Module Structure
docs.text: Module Repository Purpose prism github.com/grokify/prism Orchestrator connecting all modules prism-capability github.com/grokify/prism-capability Capability stack definitions prism-maturity github.com/grokify/prism-maturity Maturity models, SLIs, state tracking prism-roadmap github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap Goals, roadmaps, requirements

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
The PRISM ecosystem is grounded in these principles:

  1. Capability Stack = Stable Structure
  2. What the organization/system must be able to do
  3. Domain/function-first decomposition

  4. Relatively stable over time

  5. Maturity = Outcome Realization

  6. Not just "having something in place"
  7. How well it works in practice, at scale

  8. Composite of multiple dimensions

  9. Roadmap = Time-Phased Delivery

  10. Capability-to-initiative mapping
  11. Dependency-driven sequencing
  12. Two-way traceability (strategy ↔ execution)

docs.title: Maturity Dimensions
docs.text:
From the ideation analysis, maturity is multi-dimensional:

Dimension What It Measures Metric Type Operational Fitness Reliability, latency, uptime SLOs Adoption Usage, penetration, coverage Usage metrics Outcome Impact Business value, decision quality KPIs Integration Embeddedness in flows Dependency metrics Agility Time-to-change, evolution speed Lead time metrics

docs.title: Importance and Dynamic Priority
docs.text:
PRISM uses a two-tier priority system:

  1. Static Importance - Inherent weight of a capability (defined in prism-capability)
  2. Dynamic Priority (P0-P3) - Calculated from importance and maturity gap
Importance Weight Description critical 4 Critical "-ilities" (security, availability, resiliency) high 3 High importance capabilities medium 2 Standard importance (default) low 1 Nice-to-have capabilities

Priority Calculation:

Priority Score = Importance Weight × (Target Level - Current Level)
Score Priority Action Required ≥8 P0 Immediate action ≥4 P1 High priority ≥2 P2 Scheduled improvement <2 P3 Low priority

Example: - Critical capability at M2, target M4 → Score = 4 × 2 = 8 → P0 - Medium capability at M3, target M4 → Score = 2 × 1 = 2 → P2

docs.title: SLI Hierarchy
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Capability Level Objectives                  │
│         Unified threshold-based measurement framework           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                    │                    │
        ▼                    ▼                    ▼
┌───────────────┐    ┌───────────────┐    ┌───────────────┐
│     SLOs      │    │   Adoption    │    │   Outcome     │
│               │    │   Objectives  │    │   Objectives  │
│ Availability  │    │ Team coverage │    │ Business KPIs │
│ Latency       │    │ Transaction % │    │ Decision lift │
│ Error rate    │    │ API usage     │    │ Conversion    │
└───────────────┘    └───────────────┘    └───────────────┘

docs.title: prism-capability
docs.text:
Purpose: Define what capabilities exist in an organization's technology landscape.

Key Types: - CapabilityStack - Root document - Capability - Individual capability with status, dependencies, tooling - Layer - Horizontal grouping (phases, stages) - Category - Visual grouping with colors - PRISMRef - Link to maturity model

Document Example: 

{
  "metadata": { "name": "security-stack", "domain": "security" },
  "layers": [
    { "id": "shift-left", "name": "Shift-Left Security", "phase": "build" }
  ],
  "capabilities": [
    {
      "id": "sast",
      "name": "SAST",
      "layerId": "shift-left",
      "status": "operational",
      "prismRef": {
        "domainId": "security",
        "sliIds": ["sli-sast-coverage", "sli-sast-findings"]
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: prism-maturity
docs.text:
Purpose: Define how capabilities are measured and track current maturity state.

Key Types: - MaturityModel - SLI definitions, level thresholds - MaturityState - Current measurements per capability - SLI - Service Level Indicator definition - Domain - Grouping of related SLIs - LevelThreshold - M1-M5 criteria

Document Relationships: 

MaturityModel (what good looks like)
    │
    ▼
MaturityState (where we are today)
    │
    ▼
Gap Analysis (what needs improvement)

SLI Categories: | Category | Focus | Examples | |----------|-------|----------| | Operational | System health | Availability, latency, error rate | | Adoption | Usage uptake | Team coverage, transaction flow | | Outcome | Business impact | Revenue lift, risk reduction | | Quality | Decision effectiveness | Precision, recall, override rate |

docs.title: prism-roadmap
docs.text:
Purpose: Plan and track improvement initiatives aligned to capability maturity.

Key Types: - OKR - Objectives and Key Results - V2MOM - Vision, Values, Methods, Obstacles, Measures - Roadmap - Time-phased delivery plan - Initiative - Specific improvement project - PRD/MRD/TRD - Requirements documents - DMAIC - Six Sigma process improvement (migrated from structured-goals)

Capability-to-Initiative Mapping: 

{
  "initiative": "Implement SAST pipeline",
  "capabilityId": "sast",
  "maturityTarget": {
    "from": "M2",
    "to": "M4"
  },
  "phase": "Q2-2026",
  "keyResults": [
    "100% of repos have SAST enabled",
    "<24h remediation SLA for critical findings"
  ]
}

docs.title: prism (Orchestrator)
docs.text:
Purpose: Connect all modules with unified queries, cross-references, and workflows.

Key Features:

  1. Unified Document Loading 

    ecosystem, err := prism.LoadEcosystem(prism.EcosystemConfig{
    CapabilityStack: "capabilities/security.json",
    MaturityModel:   "maturity/security-model.json",
    MaturityState:   "maturity/security-state.json",
    Roadmap:         "plans/security-roadmap.json",
})

  2. Cross-Module Queries 

    // Find capabilities below target maturity
gaps := ecosystem.CapabilitiesWithMaturityGap()

// Find initiatives for a capability
initiatives := ecosystem.InitiativesForCapability("sast")

// Get full context for a capability
context := ecosystem.CapabilityContext("sast")
// Returns: capability + current maturity + target + initiatives + dependencies

  3. Gap Analysis 

    analysis := ecosystem.AnalyzeGaps()
// Returns prioritized list of capabilities needing investment
// Sorted by: strategic importance × maturity gap × dependency impact

  4. Dashboard Generation 

    dashboard := ecosystem.GenerateDashboard(prism.DashboardOptions{
    Format: "dashforge",
    Widgets: []string{"maturity-heatmap", "initiative-timeline", "gap-chart"},
})

  5. Validation Across Documents 

    errs := ecosystem.Validate()
// Checks:
// - All prismRef.sliIds exist in MaturityModel
// - All initiative.capabilityId exist in CapabilityStack
// - No orphaned measurements in MaturityState

docs.title: CLI Integration
docs.text:
Each module has its own CLI, plus unified commands in prism:

# Module CLIs
prism-capability validate capabilities.json
prism-maturity assess maturity-state.json
prism-roadmap show roadmap.json

# Unified prism CLI
prism ecosystem load --config ecosystem.yaml
prism gaps analyze --sort-by=impact
prism dashboard generate --format=html
prism validate --all

docs.title: File Organization
docs.text:
Recommended project structure:

my-org-prism/
├── capabilities/
│   ├── security.json
│   ├── platform.json
│   └── data.json
├── maturity/
│   ├── model.json          # SLI definitions, level thresholds
│   └── state.json          # Current measurements
├── plans/
│   ├── okrs/
│   │   └── fy2026-q2.json
│   ├── roadmaps/
│   │   └── security-roadmap.json
│   └── initiatives/
│       └── sast-pipeline.json
├── requirements/
│   ├── prd/
│   ├── mrd/
│   └── trd/
└── prism.yaml              # Ecosystem configuration

docs.title: From Existing Repos
docs.text: Old Location New Location Action plexusone/capability-stack-spec grokify/prism-capability ✅ Done (v0.1.0 tagged) grokify/prism grokify/prism-maturity ✅ Done grokify/structured-plan grokify/prism-roadmap ✅ Done grokify/structured-goals Merge DMAIC → prism-roadmap Pending New grokify/prism This repo

docs.title: Module Path Updates
docs.text:
Each module needs go.mod path updates:

// prism-capability/go.mod
module github.com/grokify/prism-capability

// prism-maturity/go.mod
module github.com/grokify/prism-maturity

// prism-roadmap/go.mod
module github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap

// prism/go.mod
module github.com/grokify/prism

require (
    github.com/grokify/prism-capability v0.1.0
    github.com/grokify/prism-maturity v0.x.0
    github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap v0.x.0
)

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: The prism CLI provides a unified interface to the PRISM ecosystem.

docs.title: Command Structure
docs.text: prism <module> <command> [subcommand] [flags]

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Module Description capability Capability stack operations maturity Maturity model and state operations roadmap Goals and requirements operations ecosystem Cross-module ecosystem operations site Static site generation

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate PRISM documents:

prism validate --all
prism validate document.json

docs.title: stats
docs.text:
Show repository statistics:

prism stats

docs.title: Common Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --help, -h Show help for command --version Show version information --output, -o Output file path --format, -f Output format (json, html, md)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Capability operations
prism capability list stack.json
prism capability validate stack.json

# Maturity operations
prism maturity model dashboard model.json --state state.json -o dash.html

# Roadmap operations
prism roadmap goals okr render goals.json -o goals.md

# Ecosystem operations
prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml --json

# Site generation
prism site generate --stack=./stacks/ --output=./dist
prism site generate --stack=./security/ --stack=./reliability/ --title="Dashboard"

docs.title: prism capability
docs.text: Capability stack operations for managing capability definitions.

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List capabilities in a stack:

prism capability list <stack-file>
prism capability list stack.json --format json

Flags:

Flag Description --format, -f Output format: table (default), json

docs.title: show
docs.text:
Show details for a specific capability:

prism capability show <stack-file> <capability-id>
prism capability show stack.json slo-framework

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate a capability stack:

prism capability validate <stack-file>
prism capability validate stack.json

Checks:

  • Valid JSON structure
  • Required fields present
  • Layer references resolve
  • No duplicate IDs
  • Dependency cycles detected

docs.title: init
docs.text:
Initialize a new capability stack:

prism capability init --domain <domain> --output <file>
prism capability init --domain security --output security-stack.json

Flags:

Flag Description --domain, -d Domain name for the stack --output, -o Output file path

docs.title: render
docs.text:
Render a capability stack to different formats:

prism capability render <stack-file> --format <format>
prism capability render stack.json --format markdown -o capabilities.md

Flags:

Flag Description --format, -f Output format: markdown, marp, html --output, -o Output file path

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# List all capabilities with their layers
prism capability list ops-stack.json

# Show a specific capability with its SLI links
prism capability show ops-stack.json slo-framework

# Validate before committing
prism capability validate ops-stack.json

# Create a new stack
prism capability init --domain platform --output platform-stack.json

docs.title: Capability Stack Format
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-capability/schema/capstack.schema.json",
  "metadata": {
    "domain": "operations",
    "version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "layers": [
    {
      "id": "observe",
      "name": "Observe",
      "order": 1
    }
  ],
  "capabilities": [
    {
      "id": "slo-framework",
      "name": "SLO Framework",
      "layerId": "observe",
      "status": "active",
      "prismRef": {
        "sliIds": ["sli-slo-coverage", "sli-slo-attainment"]
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: prism ecosystem
docs.text: Cross-module ecosystem operations for loading and querying across PRISM modules.

docs.title: load
docs.text:
Load an ecosystem from a configuration file or directory:

prism ecosystem load [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --config, -c Configuration file (JSON/YAML) --dir, -d Directory to load from --json Output statistics as JSON

Examples:

# Load from config file
prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml

# Load from directory structure
prism ecosystem load --dir ./ecosystem

# Output as JSON
prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml --json

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
When using --dir, the expected structure is:

ecosystem/
├── capability/
│   ├── security-stack.json
│   └── platform-stack.json
├── maturity/
│   ├── model.json
│   └── state.json
└── roadmap/
    ├── okrs/
    │   └── q1-2026.json
    └── roadmaps/
        └── 2026-roadmap.json

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
# prism.yaml
ecosystem:
  name: my-organization

  capability:
    files:
      - capabilities/security.json
      - capabilities/platform.json

  maturity:
    files:
      - maturity/model.json
      - maturity/state.json

  roadmap:
    okrs:
      - plans/okrs/q1-2026.json
    roadmaps:
      - plans/roadmaps/2026.json

docs.title: Cross-Module Queries
docs.text:
The ecosystem loader enables cross-module queries:

  • Capability Context: Get a capability with its linked SLIs/metrics
  • Validation: Validate cross-references (e.g., capability → SLI links)
  • Statistics: Aggregate counts across all modules

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
When loading an ecosystem, cross-references are validated:

Validation errors:
  - capability.slo-framework.prismRef.sliIds: references non-existent metric/SLI "sli-missing"

Validation checks:

  • Capability PRISMRef.SLIIDs reference existing metrics
  • Each capability stack is internally valid
  • Each PRISM document is internally valid

docs.title: prism maturity
docs.text: Maturity model and state operations for measuring organizational maturity.

docs.title: model dashboard
docs.text:
Generate an HTML dashboard from a maturity model:

prism maturity model dashboard <model-file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --state State document with current measurements --capstack Capability stack for layer-based views --aggregation Aggregation method: min (default), avg --format, -f Output format: html (default), json --output, -o Output file path

Examples:

# Basic dashboard
prism maturity model dashboard model.json -o dashboard.html

# With state overlay
prism maturity model dashboard model.json \
  --state state.json \
  -o dashboard.html

# With capability layer views
prism maturity model dashboard model.json \
  --state state.json \
  --capstack capabilities.json \
  --aggregation min \
  -o dashboard.html

docs.title: model validate
docs.text:
Validate a maturity model specification:

prism maturity model validate <model-file>

Checks:

  • Valid JSON structure
  • Required fields present (domains, levels, criteria)
  • SLI references resolve
  • Level numbers sequential (1-5)
  • Valid operators

docs.title: model lint
docs.text:
Lint a maturity model for common issues:

prism maturity model lint <model-file> [--strict]

Flags:

Flag Description --strict Treat warnings as errors

Checks:

  • Criteria without sliId (won't show in dashboard)
  • Missing operator or target
  • Unused SLI definitions
  • Missing units
  • Threshold coverage gaps

Exit codes:

  • 0 - No issues
  • 1 - Warnings found
  • 2 - Errors found

docs.title: Other Commands
docs.text:
# Analyze maturity gaps
prism maturity analyze <document>

# Export to different formats
prism maturity export okr <document> --output goals.json
prism maturity export v2mom <document> --output v2mom.json

# Generate reports
prism maturity report <document> --format markdown

docs.title: Dashboard Views
docs.text:
When a capability stack is provided, the dashboard includes:

  1. Layer Maturity Overview - Horizontal bar chart showing aggregate maturity per layer
  2. Layer Summary Cards - Metric cards with capability counts and maturity levels
  3. Capability Bullets - Bullet charts grouped by layer showing individual capability maturity

docs.title: Aggregation Methods
docs.text: Method Description Use Case min Minimum value across SLIs Conservative; "you're only as strong as your weakest link" avg Average value across SLIs Balanced view of overall maturity

docs.title: Model Format
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-maturity/schema/maturity-model.schema.json",
  "domains": {
    "operations": {
      "name": "Operations",
      "levels": [
        {
          "level": 1,
          "name": "Initial",
          "criteria": [
            {
              "id": "crit-availability-m1",
              "sliId": "sli-availability",
              "operator": "gte",
              "target": 95
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "slis": {
    "sli-availability": {
      "name": "Service Availability",
      "unit": "%",
      "sliType": "availability"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: prism roadmap
docs.text: Goals and requirements operations for planning and execution.

docs.title: goals
docs.text:
Work with goal documents (OKR, V2MOM):

prism roadmap goals <type> <command> [flags]

docs.title: requirements
docs.text:
Work with requirements documents (PRD, MRD, TRD):

prism roadmap requirements <type> <command> [flags]

docs.title: okr render
docs.text:
Render OKRs to different formats:

prism roadmap goals okr render <file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --format, -f Output format: markdown (default), marp, json --output, -o Output file path

Examples:

# Render to markdown
prism roadmap goals okr render q1-okrs.json -o okrs.md

# Generate Marp slides
prism roadmap goals okr render q1-okrs.json -f marp -o okrs-slides.md

docs.title: v2mom render
docs.text:
Render V2MOM to different formats:

prism roadmap goals v2mom render <file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --format, -f Output format: markdown (default), marp, json --output, -o Output file path

docs.title: prd render
docs.text:
Render a PRD to different formats:

prism roadmap requirements prd render <file> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description --format, -f Output format: markdown (default), marp, pandoc --output, -o Output file path

Examples:

# Render to markdown with YAML frontmatter
prism roadmap requirements prd render feature.json -o feature.md

# Generate Marp presentation
prism roadmap requirements prd render feature.json -f marp -o feature-slides.md

docs.title: mrd render
docs.text:
Render an MRD (Market Requirements Document):

prism roadmap requirements mrd render <file> [flags]

docs.title: trd render
docs.text:
Render a TRD (Technical Requirements Document):

prism roadmap requirements trd render <file> [flags]

docs.title: schema generate
docs.text:
Generate JSON Schema for document types:

prism roadmap schema generate <type> [flags]

Types: prd, mrd, trd, okr, v2mom

docs.title: schema validate
docs.text:
Validate a document against its schema:

prism roadmap schema validate <file>

docs.title: OKR Format
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap/schema/okr.schema.json",
  "metadata": {
    "period": "Q1 2026",
    "team": "Platform"
  },
  "objectives": [
    {
      "id": "obj-reliability",
      "title": "Improve platform reliability",
      "keyResults": [
        {
          "id": "kr-availability",
          "description": "Achieve 99.9% availability",
          "target": 99.9,
          "current": 99.5,
          "unit": "%"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: PRD Format
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap/schema/prd.schema.json",
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Feature Name",
    "author": "Product Manager",
    "status": "draft"
  },
  "problem": {
    "statement": "Description of the problem",
    "impact": "Business impact of the problem"
  },
  "solution": {
    "overview": "Proposed solution",
    "requirements": [
      {
        "id": "req-1",
        "description": "Requirement description",
        "priority": "P0"
      }
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
The typical document flow:

  1. Goals (OKR/V2MOM) define what the roadmap achieves
  2. Roadmap sequences work items (RMIs)
  3. Requirements (MRD/PRD/TRD) detail how to execute each RMI
OKR/V2MOM → Roadmap → RMI → MRD → PRD → TRD

docs.title: prism site
docs.text: Generate static websites from PRISM data.

docs.title: generate
docs.text:
Generate a multi-page static site from capability stacks, maturity models, and state documents.

prism site generate [flags]

Generated Site Structure:

  • Homepage with cards linking to all capability stacks
  • Stack pages with filterable capability grids
  • Capability detail pages with SLI metrics

docs.title: Standard PRISM Directory Structure
docs.text:
PRISM supports a standard directory structure that enables auto-discovery of related files:

{stack-name}/
├── stack.json           # Capability stack definition
├── model.json           # Maturity model (SLIs, levels, criteria)
├── state.json           # Current state (SLI values)
└── roadmap.json         # OKRs, initiatives (optional)

When using the standard structure, all files are auto-discovered:

prism site generate --stack=./security/ --stack=./reliability/

Or point to a parent directory containing multiple stack directories:

stacks/
├── security/
│   ├── stack.json
│   ├── model.json
│   └── state.json
└── reliability/
    ├── stack.json
    ├── model.json
    └── state.json

prism site generate --stack=./stacks/ --output=./dist

docs.title: Legacy Mode
docs.text:
For non-standard layouts, use --models and --states directories:

prism site generate \
  --stack=./stacks/security.json \
  --models=./models/ \
  --states=./states/ \
  --output=./dist

Model/state files are matched by naming convention:

  • {basename}-model.json or {basename}/model.json
  • {basename}-state.json or {basename}/state.json

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Default Description --stack (required) Capability stack file(s) or directory (can be repeated) --output, -o ./dist Output directory for generated site --title PRISM Dashboard Site title --description Capability maturity dashboard Site description --theme dark Theme: light or dark --base-url Base URL for the site (for absolute links) --models Directory containing maturity model JSON files (legacy mode) --states Directory containing state JSON files (legacy mode) --site-nav-js Path to site-nav JavaScript bundle

docs.title: Single standard stack directory
docs.text: prism site generate --stack=./security/ --output=./dist

docs.title: Multiple standard stack directories
docs.text:
prism site generate \
  --stack=./security/ \
  --stack=./reliability/ \
  --output=./dist \
  --title="PRISM Dashboard"

docs.title: Parent directory with multiple stacks
docs.text:
prism site generate \
  --stack=./stacks/ \
  --output=./dist/ \
  --theme=dark

docs.title: Legacy: separate files with models/states directories
docs.text:
prism site generate \
  --stack=./security-stack.json \
  --models=./models/ \
  --states=./states/ \
  --output=./dist

docs.title: With site-nav web components
docs.text:
prism site generate \
  --stack=./stacks/ \
  --output=./dist \
  --site-nav-js=./node_modules/@grokify/site-nav/dist/site-nav.es.js

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
The generated site follows this structure:

dist/
├── index.html              # Homepage with stack cards
├── assets/
│   └── styles.css          # Theme-aware CSS
├── {stack-basename}/
│   ├── index.html          # Stack overview with capability grid
│   └── {capability-id}.html  # Capability detail pages
└── site-nav.es.js          # (optional) Web components bundle

docs.title: Theming
docs.text:
The generated site supports both light and dark themes. The default theme is dark.

When using the site-nav JavaScript bundle, users can toggle between themes using the theme toggle control in the navigation.

Without the JavaScript bundle, the site uses CSS-only theming with graceful fallbacks for badges and status indicators.

docs.title: CSS Custom Properties
docs.text:
The site uses CSS custom properties for theming. Override these in your own CSS:

:root {
  --ds-bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --ds-text-primary: #1e293b;
  --ds-accent: #06b6d4;
}

[theme="dark"] {
  --ds-bg-primary: #0f172a;
  --ds-text-primary: #f1f5f9;
  --ds-accent: #22d3ee;
}

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later
  • Git (for cloning repositories)

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/prism/cmd/prism@latest

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text: prism --version

docs.title: Install Individual Modules
docs.text:
If you only need specific functionality, you can install standalone CLIs:

# Capability stack CLI
go install github.com/grokify/prism-capability/cmd/capstack@latest

# Maturity model CLI
go install github.com/grokify/prism-maturity/cmd/prism@latest

# Roadmap/planning CLI
go install github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap/cmd/splan@latest

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
For contributing or local development:

# Clone the ecosystem
mkdir -p ~/go/src/github.com/grokify
cd ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

git clone https://github.com/grokify/prism-core.git
git clone https://github.com/grokify/prism-capability.git
git clone https://github.com/grokify/prism-maturity.git
git clone https://github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap.git
git clone https://github.com/grokify/prism.git

# Build umbrella CLI
cd prism
go build ./cmd/prism

The umbrella repo uses replace directives in go.mod to reference local sibling directories during development.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks through the PRISM document flow from capability definition to execution planning.

docs.title: Document Flow Overview
docs.text: Capability Stack  →  Maturity Model + State  →  OKR/V2MOM + Roadmap  →  MRD/PRD/TRD

docs.title: Step 1: Define Capabilities
docs.text:
Create a capability stack defining what your organization needs:

# Initialize a capability stack
prism capability init --domain security --output capabilities.json

# Validate the stack
prism capability validate capabilities.json

# List capabilities
prism capability list capabilities.json

docs.title: Step 2: Define Maturity Model
docs.text:
Create a maturity model with SLIs and level criteria:

# Generate a maturity dashboard
prism maturity model dashboard model.json --output dashboard.html

# Validate the model
prism maturity model validate model.json

# Lint for common issues
prism maturity model lint model.json

docs.title: Step 3: Track Current State
docs.text:
Create a state document with current measurements:

# Generate dashboard with state overlay
prism maturity model dashboard model.json \
  --state state.json \
  --output dashboard.html

docs.title: Step 4: Link Capabilities to SLIs
docs.text:
Add PRISMRef to capabilities to link them to maturity SLIs:

{
  "id": "slo-framework",
  "name": "SLO Framework",
  "prismRef": {
    "sliIds": ["sli-slo-coverage", "sli-slo-attainment"]
  }
}

Then generate a dashboard with capability layer views:

prism maturity model dashboard model.json \
  --state state.json \
  --capstack capabilities.json \
  --aggregation min \
  --output dashboard.html

docs.title: Step 5: Define Goals
docs.text:
Create OKRs or V2MOMs to define success:

# Render OKRs to markdown
prism roadmap goals okr render okrs.json --output okrs.md

# Render V2MOM
prism roadmap goals v2mom render v2mom.json --output v2mom.md

docs.title: Step 6: Create Requirements
docs.text:
For each roadmap item, create detailed requirements:

# Render PRD to markdown
prism roadmap requirements prd render feature.json --output feature-prd.md

# Generate Marp slides
prism roadmap requirements prd render feature.json --format marp --output feature.md

docs.title: Step 7: Generate Static Site
docs.text:
Generate a multi-page static website from your PRISM data:

# Using standard PRISM directory structure
prism site generate --stack=./security/ --output=./dist

# Multiple stacks
prism site generate \
  --stack=./security/ \
  --stack=./reliability/ \
  --output=./dist \
  --title="PRISM Dashboard"

The generated site includes:

  • Homepage with cards linking to all capability stacks
  • Stack pages with filterable capability grids
  • Capability detail pages with SLI metrics

docs.title: Standard PRISM Directory Structure
docs.text:
PRISM supports a standard directory structure that enables auto-discovery:

{stack-name}/
├── stack.json           # Capability stack definition
├── model.json           # Maturity model (SLIs, levels, criteria)
├── state.json           # Current state (SLI values)
└── roadmap.json         # OKRs, initiatives (optional)

When using this structure, files are auto-discovered:

# Single stack
prism site generate --stack=./security/

# Parent directory with multiple stacks
prism site generate --stack=./stacks/ --output=./dist

docs.title: Loading an Ecosystem
docs.text:
Once you have all documents, load them as an ecosystem:

# From config file
prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml

# From directory structure
prism ecosystem load --dir ./ecosystem

Expected ecosystem directory structure:

ecosystem/
├── capability/
│   └── *.json
├── maturity/
│   └── *.json
└── roadmap/
    ├── okrs/*.json
    └── roadmaps/*.json

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• CLI Reference - Full command documentation
  • Configuration - Ecosystem configuration options
  • Integration Guide - Cross-module workflows

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: This guide covers how to configure PRISM ecosystem documents and the umbrella CLI.

docs.title: YAML Format
docs.text:
# prism.yaml
ecosystem:
  name: my-organization

  capability:
    files:
      - capabilities/security-stack.json
      - capabilities/platform-stack.json

  maturity:
    files:
      - maturity/operations-model.json
      - maturity/operations-state.json

  roadmap:
    okrs:
      - plans/okrs/q1-2026.json
      - plans/okrs/q2-2026.json
    roadmaps:
      - plans/roadmaps/2026-roadmap.json

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
{
  "name": "my-organization",
  "capability": {
    "files": [
      "capabilities/security-stack.json",
      "capabilities/platform-stack.json"
    ]
  },
  "maturity": {
    "files": [
      "maturity/operations-model.json",
      "maturity/operations-state.json"
    ]
  },
  "roadmap": {
    "okrs": ["plans/okrs/q1-2026.json"],
    "roadmaps": ["plans/roadmaps/2026-roadmap.json"]
  }
}

docs.title: Document Schemas
docs.text: All PRISM documents support JSON Schema validation. Reference the schema in your documents:

docs.title: Capability Stack
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-capability/schema/capstack.schema.json",
  "metadata": {
    "domain": "security",
    "version": "1.0.0"
  }
}

docs.title: Maturity Model
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-maturity/schema/maturity-model.schema.json",
  "domains": {}
}

docs.title: PRD
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-roadmap/schema/prd.schema.json",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: Capability → SLI Links
docs.text:
Capabilities link to maturity SLIs via PRISMRef:

{
  "id": "slo-framework",
  "name": "SLO Framework",
  "prismRef": {
    "sliIds": ["sli-slo-coverage", "sli-slo-attainment"]
  }
}

docs.title: Roadmap Item → Requirements
docs.text:
Roadmap items reference requirement documents:

{
  "id": "rmi-auth-redesign",
  "title": "Authentication Redesign",
  "requirements": {
    "mrd": "requirements/auth-mrd.json",
    "prd": "requirements/auth-prd.json",
    "trd": "requirements/auth-trd.json"
  }
}

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Default PRISM_CONFIG Default config file path prism.yaml PRISM_OUTPUT_DIR Default output directory .

docs.title: VS Code
docs.text:
Add JSON Schema associations in .vscode/settings.json:

{
  "json.schemas": [
    {
      "fileMatch": ["**/capabilities/*.json"],
      "url": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-capability/schema/capstack.schema.json"
    },
    {
      "fileMatch": ["**/maturity/model*.json"],
      "url": "https://github.com/grokify/prism-maturity/schema/maturity-model.schema.json"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Recommended Directory Structure
docs.text:
project/
├── prism.yaml                    # Ecosystem config
├── capabilities/
│   ├── security-stack.json
│   └── platform-stack.json
├── maturity/
│   ├── model.json                # Maturity model definition
│   └── state-q1-2026.json        # Current state snapshot
├── plans/
│   ├── okrs/
│   │   ├── q1-2026.json
│   │   └── q2-2026.json
│   ├── roadmaps/
│   │   └── 2026-roadmap.json
│   └── requirements/
│       ├── feature-a-prd.json
│       └── feature-b-prd.json
└── output/
    └── dashboard.html

docs.title: Integration Guide
docs.text: This guide covers how to integrate PRISM modules for end-to-end planning workflows.

docs.title: Document Flow
docs.text:
Capability Stack          What capabilities do we need?
       │
       ▼
Maturity Model + State    Where are we for each capability?
       │
       ▼
OKR/V2MOM + Roadmap       What goals and sequence to improve?
       │
       ▼
MRD / PRD / TRD           How do we execute each roadmap item?

docs.title: 1. Start with Capabilities
docs.text:
Define your capability stack first. This is the stable foundation:

# Create capability stack
prism capability init --domain operations --output ops-stack.json

# Edit to add layers and capabilities
# ...

# Validate
prism capability validate ops-stack.json

Key decisions:

  • What layers organize your capabilities? (e.g., Observe, Respond, Recover)
  • What capabilities exist at each layer?
  • What are the dependencies between capabilities?

docs.title: 2. Define Maturity Model
docs.text:
Create a maturity model with SLIs that measure each capability:

{
  "domains": {
    "operations": {
      "levels": [
        {
          "level": 1,
          "criteria": [
            {
              "sliId": "sli-availability",
              "operator": "gte",
              "target": 95
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "slis": {
    "sli-availability": {
      "name": "Service Availability",
      "unit": "%"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 3. Link Capabilities to SLIs
docs.text:
Add PRISMRef to capabilities:

{
  "id": "observability-platform",
  "name": "Observability Platform",
  "prismRef": {
    "sliIds": [
      "sli-monitoring-coverage",
      "sli-alerting-quality"
    ]
  }
}

docs.title: 4. Track Current State
docs.text:
Create a state document with current measurements:

{
  "sliState": {
    "sli-availability": {
      "windows": {
        "30d": { "value": 99.5 }
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: 5. Generate Maturity Dashboard
docs.text:
View maturity with capability layer grouping:

prism maturity model dashboard model.json \
  --state state.json \
  --capstack ops-stack.json \
  --aggregation min \
  -o dashboard.html

docs.title: 6. Identify Gaps
docs.text:
The dashboard shows:

  • Current maturity level per SLI
  • Target level (M5)
  • Capabilities with lowest maturity (improvement opportunities)

docs.title: 7. Create Goals
docs.text:
Based on gaps, create OKRs:

{
  "objectives": [
    {
      "id": "obj-reliability",
      "title": "Improve platform reliability to M4",
      "keyResults": [
        {
          "description": "Achieve 99.9% availability",
          "target": 99.9,
          "current": 99.5
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: 8. Build Roadmap
docs.text:
Sequence work items to achieve goals:

{
  "phases": [
    {
      "id": "q1-2026",
      "name": "Q1 2026",
      "items": [
        {
          "id": "rmi-monitoring-upgrade",
          "title": "Upgrade monitoring coverage",
          "capabilityId": "observability-platform"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: 9. Create Requirements
docs.text:
For each roadmap item, create detailed specs:

# Market requirements
prism roadmap requirements mrd render rmi-monitoring-mrd.json -o mrd.md

# Product requirements
prism roadmap requirements prd render rmi-monitoring-prd.json -o prd.md

# Technical requirements
prism roadmap requirements trd render rmi-monitoring-trd.json -o trd.md

docs.title: Cross-Module Validation
docs.text:
Load the full ecosystem to validate cross-references:

prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml

This validates:

  • Capability → SLI references exist
  • Roadmap item → Capability references exist
  • All documents are structurally valid

docs.title: CI/CD Integration
docs.text:
Add validation to your CI pipeline:

# .github/workflows/prism-validate.yaml
name: PRISM Validation
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.21'
      - run: go install github.com/grokify/prism/cmd/prism@latest
      - run: prism capability validate capabilities/*.json
      - run: prism maturity model validate maturity/model.json
      - run: prism ecosystem load --config prism.yaml

docs.title: Dashboard Generation
docs.text:
Generate dashboards on state updates:

#!/bin/bash
# generate-dashboard.sh
prism maturity model dashboard \
  maturity/model.json \
  --state maturity/state.json \
  --capstack capabilities/ops-stack.json \
  --aggregation min \
  -o docs/dashboard.html

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Version your documents - Include version in metadata
  2. Validate on commit - Use pre-commit hooks or CI
  3. Keep capabilities stable - They change less often than state
  4. Snapshot state regularly - Track maturity over time (e.g., state-q1-2026.json)
  5. Link everything - Use PRISMRef and document references

docs.title: v0.7.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-05-18

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This release marks a major restructuring of the PRISM repository. The maturity modeling functionality previously in this repository has been moved to prism-intelligence, and this repository now serves as the unified orchestration layer for the PRISM ecosystem.

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
Repository Repurposed

The previous github.com/grokify/prism functionality (maturity modeling, SLIs, SLOs) is now in github.com/grokify/prism-intelligence. Update your imports accordingly.

Migration:

// Before (v0.6.0 and earlier)
import "github.com/grokify/prism"

// After (v0.7.0+)
import "github.com/grokify/prism-intelligence"

docs.title: Ecosystem Orchestrator
docs.text:
The prism repository now provides a unified orchestration layer that integrates:

  • prism-capability - Capability stacks and dependencies
  • prism-intelligence - SLIs, SLOs, and maturity models
  • prism-execution - OKRs, roadmaps, and initiatives

docs.title: Cross-Module Queries
docs.text:
import "github.com/grokify/prism/ecosystem"

eco := ecosystem.New(config)

// Get full context for a capability
ctx := eco.GetCapabilityContext("sast")

// Query all metrics across documents
metrics := eco.AllMetrics()

// Query all initiatives across roadmaps
initiatives := eco.AllInitiatives()

docs.title: Ecosystem Types
docs.text:
• Config - Configuration for loading ecosystem files
  • Ecosystem - Aggregates all PRISM module documents
  • Statistics - Summary statistics across modules

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Module Version prism-core v0.1.0 prism-capability v0.3.0 prism-intelligence v0.9.0 prism-execution v0.12.0

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/grokify/prism@v0.7.0

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository
  • Ecosystem Design
  • Changelog

docs.title: v0.8.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-06-07

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Unified CLI - Single CLI integrating all PRISM ecosystem modules
  • Static Site Generator - HTML dashboards for capability maturity visualization
  • Lit Web Components - Interactive maturity-grid component for web visualization
  • Module Renames - prism-intelligence → prism-maturity, prism-execution → prism-roadmap

docs.title: Unified CLI
docs.text:
The prism CLI now integrates commands from all ecosystem modules:

# Capability stack commands (from prism-capability)
prism capability list stack.json
prism capability show stack.json sast
prism capability render stack.json --format html

# Maturity commands (from prism-maturity)
prism maturity model report model.json
prism maturity state show state.json
prism maturity plan dashboard plan.json

# Roadmap commands (from prism-roadmap)
prism roadmap okr list okrs.json
prism roadmap v2mom convert doc.json
prism roadmap mrd generate spec.json

# Ecosystem commands
prism ecosystem load --dir ./ecosystem
prism validate --config prism.yaml
prism stats --dir ./ecosystem --json

docs.title: Static Site Generator
docs.text:
Generate HTML dashboards from capability stacks with maturity overlays:

# Generate site from standard directory structure
prism site generate --stack=./security/ --output=./dist

# Multiple stacks with theme
prism site generate \
  --stack=./security/ \
  --stack=./reliability/ \
  --output=./dist \
  --theme=dark

# With layer-based aggregation
prism site generate \
  --stack=./stacks/ \
  --aggregation=min \
  --output=./dist

Features:

  • Homepage with cards linking to all capability stacks
  • Filterable capability grids per stack
  • Capability detail pages with SLI metrics
  • Maturity level badges (M1-M5) with color coding
  • Metrics count indicator on capability cards
  • Layer-based aggregation views (min/avg methods)
  • Light and dark theme support

docs.title: Lit Web Components
docs.text:
New @prism/ui TypeScript package with Lit-based web components for interactive visualization:

# Generate site with Lit component integration
prism site generate \
  --stack=./security/ \
  --output=./dist \
  --prism-ui-js=./ui/dist/prism-ui.js

maturity-grid Component:

<maturity-grid
  view="by-layer"
  theme="dark"
  show-legend
  show-view-toggle
  base-url="./capabilities/">
  <script type="application/json">
    {"layers": [...], "capabilities": [...], "maturity": {...}}
  </script>
</maturity-grid>

Features:

  • Interactive capability grid with maturity overlay
  • Toggle between by-layer and by-category views
  • Theme-aware rendering (light/dark mode sync)
  • Maturity levels M1-M5 with color coding
  • Legend display with level descriptions
  • Click-through to capability detail pages

Building the UI:

cd ui/
npm install
npm run build  # outputs dist/prism-ui.js

docs.title: Module Renames
docs.text:
The ecosystem modules have been renamed for clarity:

Old Name New Name Description prism-intelligence prism-maturity SLIs, SLOs, maturity models prism-execution prism-roadmap OKRs, V2MOMs, roadmaps

Config types updated accordingly:

  • IntelligenceConfig → MaturityConfig
  • ExecutionConfig → RoadmapConfig

docs.title: Package Rename
docs.text:
The site/ package has been renamed to sitegen/ to avoid conflict with MkDocs output directory.

import "github.com/grokify/prism/sitegen"

config := sitegen.Config{
    StackPaths: []string{"./security/"},
    OutputDir:  "./dist",
    Theme:      "dark",
}
gen := sitegen.NewGenerator(config)
gen.Generate()

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: Module Version Change prism-capability v0.5.0 Updated prism-maturity v0.11.0 Updated (renamed from prism-intelligence) prism-roadmap v0.14.0 Updated (renamed from prism-execution) prism-core v0.2.0 Updated structured-evaluation v0.7.0 Updated priority-frameworks v0.2.0 Added spf13/cobra v1.10.2 For CLI lit 3.2.0 For web components (ui/)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/prism/cmd/prism@v0.8.0

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
New documentation added:

  • Getting Started - Installation and quickstart
  • CLI Reference - Command reference for all modules
  • Configuration Guide - Ecosystem configuration
  • Integration Guide - Module integration patterns

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository
  • v0.7.0 Release Notes - Ecosystem orchestrator
  • Changelog
